def: s.m. One who values, &c.; appraiser ( = ankak); an officer employed to survey and appraise standing crops.

def: = H انکورا अंकूरा aṅkūrā, = aṅkur, aṅkurā, q.q.v.

= H انکور अंकूर aṅkūr, = aṅkur, aṅkurā, q.q.v.

def: s.m. The iron hook with which elephants are driven; goad; anything or person that acts as a spur or impels to action, &c.
more: n. m. S. अङ्कुश, Pr. aṅkuso, aṅko . 1. The iron-hook with which elephants are driven; a goad. Hātī kā pīr aṅkas . Prov. The goad is the elephant's master. 2. A director; governor; master; teacher. 1. Bachche ke ūpar koī aṅkas nahīṅ, din bhar kheltā phirtā hai . Wom. 2. Man kā aṅkus gyān . Prov. Conscience is the goad of the mind.
subs: v.t. To goad; to reduce to submission, bring to obedience.

def: s.m. The iron hook with which elephants are driven; goad; anything or person that acts as a spur or impels to action, &c.

def: s.f. (dim. of aṅkus, q.v.), Hook, tenter.

def: n. m. S. अन्न food, कूट heap. A festival celebrated by Hindus, in commemoration of Krishna's sacrifice, on the day following the Divālī. ☞ It consists in offering up at a temple dedicated to Vishnu, a large mess composed of every variety of cooked grain, vegetables. etc. A pinch of this mess is given by the pujārī or temple priest to each worshipper who finds in it some one article of food from which he or she had religiously abstained for a certain period in fulfilment of a vow made for the sake of some such blessing as the removal of barrenness, etc. an na suhā'nā,— —bhānā , v. n. To be indifferent, or averse to food. Magan bhaī chhabi nirakhi ke, hameṅ na ann suhāë . Song. Enraptured with the beauteous One, desire for food or drink I've none.
s.m. = aṅkak and āṅkū, q.q.v. s.m. Arithmetician; accountant.
v.t. To cause to value, &c.; also = aṅkānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to be valued, to have or get (a thing) valued or appraised; to value, appraise; ( = āṅknā, q.v.); to examine (as cloth); to approve of.
s.f. Embrace; bosom
more: s. f. Embrace, bosom. انکوار بهرنا ankwār bharnā, To embrace.
subs: To take into, or to fill, the embrace; to embrace.

def: adj. Out of sorts, indisposed; dejected, sad, agitated, troubled in mind; dull.
more: adj. S. मन mind. Indisposed; dull; low-spirited; heart-sick; sad; dejected; troubled in mind. Un bin jiā an-manā . Song.
adj. mas. (for उन्मनाः ) Thoughtful, agitated, regretting, out of humour, sad.

more: v. n. (dakh.) To be perplexed, doubtful or agitated (in mind), to be sad or unwilling, to demur ( Harris ).

def: s. f. (dakh.) Perplexity, doubt.
adj. fem. (for उन्मनाः ) Thoughtful, agitated, regretting, out of humour, sad.

adj. Unmixed, without alloy, unadulterated, pure, genuine, disconnected, not related; not agreeing or harmonizing, discordant, ill-assorted, unsuitable, jarring; heterogeneous; out of proportion, disproportioned; unseasonable; foreign (to)
more: adj. S. अ not, मिल् to mix. 1. Unmixed; unalloyed; unadulterated (kharā) . 2. Clear; pure; genuine ( asl ). 3. Out of place; unrelated; unconnected; unseasonable; untimely. 4. Not matching; misjoined; ill-assorted; unsuitable. 5. Disagreeing; inapt; inapplicable ( bejoṛ ). 6. Irrelevant; foreign, or alien to. Phr. Foreign to the purpose; having nothing to do with; beside the question; brought in by the head and shoulder. 7. Unharmonious; discordant; dissonant; out of tune; unmusical. 8. Disproportioned; ill-proportioned; unsymmetrical. an-mel bāteṅ , n. f. Rambling, irrelevant, incoherent speech; nonsense; gibberish.

def: adj. Ill-assorted, unsuitable, &c. = an-mel, q.v.; — s.f. A kind of composition in which ludicrous and absurd combinations are presented in a humorous light; ludicrous misjoinders.
more: n. f. A kind of composition in which grotesque and impossible combinations are presented in a humorous light; ludicrous or witty misjoinders, as— Chūle pīchhe hal dharā, maiṅ jānūṅ baṛī lauṅg . A plough behind the fire-place—I took it for a large clove.

def: adj. Priceless, invaluable; very valuable; precious.
more: adj. Invaluable; beyond all price; priceless; precious; valuable. 1. Sīne par donoṅ chhātyāṅ an-mol, Uṅchī, chiknī, kaṛī, karārī, gol . Shauq. Two priceless glowing orbs her bosom heaved profound, The velvet's gloss, high-swe'ling, firm, and tight, and round. 2. Laiṅhgā merā lākh ṭake kā, sun le ham se bol, Choli haigī chār mohar kī, joban hai an-mol . Song. Upon my skirt I value set ten thousand pounds, Moreover hearken well, and mark you this my speech— Four mohars gold the vest my bosom's charms surrounds, My full-formed breasts, be sure, no gold untold can reach.

def: adj. Having the face turned away or averted; — adv. With the face averted.

an-kuṭ, s.m. A festival celebrated by the Hindūs (in commemoration of Krishna's sacrifice) on the day following the Dīwālī by the offering of a large quantity of mixed meats in a temple dedicated to Vishnu. (A morsel of this mess is given by the officiating priest to each worshipper, who finds therein some special article of food from which he or she had vowed temporarily to abstain in the hope of obtaining some desired blessing)
v.n. To derive no pleasure from food, to be indifferent, or averse, to food
adj. Destitute of food, or of subsistence; needy.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀆𑀯𑁂𑀇 (āvei), from Sanskrit आपयति (āpayati), from आप् (āp) + -अयति (-ayati). The perfective form آیا (āyā) may be derived from Sanskrit आगत (ā́gata, “arrived”).

def: adj. 1. Unjust ( be-insāf ); unfair; inequitable. 2. Unfeeling; hard-hearted ( kaṭhor ); cruel. 3. Tyrannical; oppressive. 4. Vicious; wicked; sinful; unprincipled; unrighteous; very bad. 5. Foul; gross; vile. 6. Flagrant; flagitious; villainous. 7. Felonious; nefarious; heinous. 8. Impious; profane; sacrilegious.
adj. (f. -ī), Uncommon, rare, wonderful, extraordinary, unprecedented; unique, singular, peculiar, novel, new, strange ( = anūṭhā); low, base, mean.
more: adj. H. an not, Panj. vekh , H. dekh see. 1. Singular; uncommon; rare. 1. Tumhārī āsā lagī hai nis din, tumhāre darshan ko tarseṅ nainā, Dulāre sundar, anūṭhe abran, haṭīle mohan, anokhe lālā . Nazīr. In him alone my hope all night and day, my thirsting eyes stretched out to see, A beauteous youth, none such adorned, perversely winning and unique is he. 2. Sab se sab bāt judā, sab se anokhī raftār, Sab se poshāk alag, sab se khichāvat khāsī , Wom. Raṅgīṅ. Throughout unique, a graceful carriage all her own, A style of dress distinct, her rounded bust tight-drawn. 2. New; novel; peculiar; unmatched. 1. Kuchh-ek tamkanat, aur kuchh-ek bāṅk-pan, Garaz har tarah meṅ anokhī phaban . Mīr Hasan. A something stately, and a something stylish too, In fine her charms became her quite unique and true. 2. Pahelī hai dadā kī bāt kuchh būjhī nahīṅ jātī, Anoṭhī hī kiyā kartī hai woh taqrīr āë din . Wom. …
(see انوٿها) adj. Rare, uncommon, wonderful. (Generally applied to man.)
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀡𑁄𑀓𑁆𑀔 (aṇokkha), *𑀅𑀡𑀯𑁂𑀓𑁆𑀔 (*aṇavekkha), from Sanskrit अनपेक्ष (anapekṣa). Compare Punjabi اَݨوکھا (aṇokhā) / ਅਣੋਖਾ (aṇokhā) and Saraiki اَنوکْھڑا (anokhṛā).

more: adj. 1. Contrary to custom, rule, usage or practice. 2. Contrary to good manners or etiquette; unmannerly; ill-behaved.

def: s.m. Part, portion share division; degree of lat. or long.). degree, grade (of a circle = darja); numerator of a fraction; right, possession. (For compounds see anś.)
more: n. m. S. अंश a part. Pr. anso, amso . 1. Portion; separate share; quota ( hissā ). 2. ( Math .) A degree of a circle. 3. ( Geog .) A degree of latitude or longitude. 4. ( Arith .) The numerator of a fraction (P. shumār-kunindah ). 4. Pith; essence; marrow; substance. 1. Is kā ans nikāl lo . 2. Abhī gehūṅ meṅ ans nahīṅ paṛā . 5. Strength; vigour; energy; vital force. Hamārā to ans nikal gayā . 6. Semen.
s. m. 1. Part, division, portion, share; right, possession, essence. 2. A degree of a circle, &c.

s.m. Tear, tears
more: n. m. S. अश्रु Pr. ansū , Pālī. aṡsu . Tears. Āṅsū ek nahīṅ, kalejā ṭūk ṭūk! Prov. Not a tear in her eye, yet (if you believe her) her heart is breaking. āṅsū ānā, bahnā, yāṭapak paṛnā , v. n. To shed or drop tears; to have tears in one's eyes. 1. Par mūī sāsī, esoṅ āë āṅsū! Prov. Her mother-in-law she lost twelve months ago, And now the tears for her begin to flow! 2. Aṅsuā bahe jaise nadyā savan kī, Nā likhī saiyāṅ patyā avan kī . Wom. Song. Birhā. As July streams my tears do run, No tidings of my love yet known. āṅsū bahā'nā, yā ḍhalkānā, ṭasve —, v. a. To shed tears or pretend to weep. Kis ne mārā jo āṅsū bahātī āī? Wom. āṅsū bhar lānā , v. n. To bring tears into the eyes; to weep. āṅsū bahnā , v. n. To flow (tears); to weep. Nainan sūṅ aṅsuvā baheṅ, mukh bachan kahyo nahīṅ jāë . Song. Birah . The tear drops from my eyes do flow, my sorrow deep no tongue can show. āṅsū puchh'nā , v. …
subs: v.n. lit. 'Tears to come'; to have tears in the eyes, to weep
To make tears flow; to shed tears; to pretend to weep
To have the eyes filled with tears, to weep
āṅsū ṭapak-paṛnā, v.n. lit. 'Tears to flow, or to drop'; to weep
v.n. To have (one's) tears wiped away; to be comforted or soothed
(-ke), To wipe away the tears (of), dry (one's) tears; to console, comfort, soothe
āṅsū ḍhalak, s.f. Running or watering of the eyes (a disease in horses, supposed to arise from a coil of hair in the outer corner of the eye)
lit. 'To wash the face with tears'; to shed tears copiously = āṭh āṭh āṅsū ronā, q.v.s.v. āṭh.
ex: اُس کی آن٘کھوں میں آن٘سُو بَھر آئے۔
us kī ā̃khõ mẽ ā̃sū bhar āe.
Her eyes were filled with tears.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀅𑀁𑀲𑀼 (aṃsu), from Sanskrit अश्रु॑ (áśru), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Háćru, from Proto-Indo-European *h₂eḱru, from *h₂eḱ-. Cognates include Gujarati આંસુ (ā̃su), Marathi आसू (āsū), Nepali आँसु (ā̃su), Punjabi ہنجھو / ਹੰਝੂ (hañjhū), and Sindhi هَنجَ (hañja).

s.m. End, limit, term, boundary, border; period, utmost extent, whole amount; completion, conclusion, termination; issue, result, consequence; end of life, death, dissolution, destruction; end in view, aim, intention, purpose; definite ascertainment, certain knowledge, certainty; knowledge or intelligence (of), account (of), news; inner part, inside, life, soul, mind, heart; nature, essence; condition; proximity; — adj. Last, final, ultimate; — adv. (S. ante), In the end, at last, finally, after all ( = ant-ko and ant-meṅ)
more: adj. from S. अन्त् to bind. 1. Final; ultimate; last. Ant matā, so matā . Prov. The last conclusion is the best. 2. Not lasting; mortal; perishable. Yeh deh ant hai .

n. m. End See اَخر (1, 2, 3). Suāṅsā. suāṅsā Krishn raṭ, suāṅsā birthā na kho, Nā jānūṅ yā suāṅs ko yehī ant nā ho . Doha. With every breath on Krishna call, Let not one breath all bootless fall, Each breath may be the last of all. 1. The utmost extent; farthest degree; limit; period. 2.
